title: Interpret Chute Opening Timing During An AGV Exchange
summary: Reference guidance for understanding documented chute opening timing during an AGV tote exchange. The source states that the chute opens when the AGV moves in, not when it drops a tote, and explains that this behavior is intentional to support faster exchanges and strict KPI targets.
when_to_use: Use this reference when observing or reviewing AGV tote exchange behavior and you need to confirm whether chute opening timing matches the documented training explanation.
not_for: Not for defining corrective actions if timing differs from the source description.
Not for inferring timing thresholds, control logic details, or light-status meanings beyond what the source states.
Not for operating, maintenance, or fault-recovery steps not described in this source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe the AGV exchange, compare observed chute timing to the documented training explanation, and record whether behavior matches the source-described intent.

safety_notes: Use observation only; the source does not provide intervention steps.
Do not infer corrective actions or timing thresholds beyond what the source states.
access_or_tools_needed: Visibility of chute behavior during AGV exchange
Source training material describing chute timing

access_required: Ability to observe an AGV tote exchange
system_preconditions: An AGV exchange is occurring or available for review
The observer can see chute behavior relative to AGV movement
external_references: UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1, segment training_video_day1_segment_0015

visual_references.description: Training video frame describing AGV tote exchange flow and stating that the chute opens when the AGV moves in, not when it drops a tote.

steps.title: Observe chute timing during the AGV exchange
steps.purpose: Establish what the chute does relative to AGV movement during the tote exchange.
steps.instruction: Observe the tote exchange and watch for when the chute opens relative to AGV movement.
steps.expected_result: The observer can identify the point in the exchange when the chute opens.
steps.healthy_condition: The chute opening can be observed relative to AGV movement into position.
steps.failure_condition: The chute timing cannot be clearly observed or appears inconsistent with the documented exchange behavior.

actions.label: Observe exchange timing
actions.exact_action: Observe the tote exchange and watch for when the chute opens relative to AGV movement.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look at the exchange sequence description and chute timing explanation tied to AGV move-in behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if chute timing does not match the documented behavior in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: We open the chute when the AGV moves in, not when it drops a tote.

steps.title: Check for chute opening on AGV move-in
steps.purpose: Verify the specific documented timing relationship described in the source.
steps.instruction: Check whether the chute opens when the AGV moves into position rather than waiting until the tote is dropped.
steps.expected_result: The observer can determine whether chute opening aligns with AGV move-in rather than tote drop.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed chute opening occurs when the AGV moves in, not only after tote drop.
steps.failure_condition: Observed chute opening appears to wait for tote drop or otherwise differs from the documented timing.

actions.label: Verify move-in timing
actions.exact_action: Check whether the chute opens when the AGV moves into position rather than waiting until the tote is dropped.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the statement that chute opening occurs when the AGV moves in, not when it drops a tote.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if chute timing does not match the documented behavior in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: We open the chute when the AGV moves in, not when it drops a tote.

steps.title: Compare observation to documented behavior
steps.purpose: Determine whether the observed exchange behavior matches the training source.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed timing to the documented behavior that chute opening occurs when the AGV moves in.
steps.expected_result: The observer can state whether the observed timing matches the documented behavior.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed timing matches the source description of chute opening during AGV move-in.
steps.failure_condition: Observed timing does not match the source description or cannot be reconciled to the documented behavior.

actions.label: Compare to source
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed timing to the documented behavior that chute opening occurs when the AGV moves in.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the exchange-process frame as the reference for documented chute timing behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if chute timing does not match the documented behavior in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: We open the chute when the AGV moves in, not when it drops a tote.

steps.title: Record the documented intent of the timing
steps.purpose: Capture that the observed timing is described by the source as intentional and KPI-driven.
steps.instruction: Record that this timing is intentional and is described as supporting strict KPI targets for faster exchanges.
steps.expected_result: The observer documents that the timing is intentional per the source and tied to exchange-speed KPI expectations.
steps.healthy_condition: The timing is understood and recorded as intentional behavior intended to speed up exchanges.
steps.failure_condition: The timing is treated as unexplained or corrective action is inferred beyond the source.

actions.label: Document intent
actions.exact_action: Record that this timing is intentional and is described as supporting strict KPI targets for faster exchanges.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the explanation that the behavior is on purpose to speed up exchanges and meet strict KPI targets.

stop_or_escalate_if: Do not infer corrective actions or timing thresholds beyond what the source states.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: We have very strict quick KPI's we have to meet. So it's, it's not, you know, if if you see the light go off and it hasn't dropped its tote, that's on purpose because we're trying to speed up the exchanges.

success_criteria: The observer confirms whether chute opening timing matches the documented behavior.
The observer understands that chute opening on AGV move-in is described as intentional.
The KPI-related reason for the timing is recorded without adding unsupported interpretation.
healthy_conditions: Chute opening is observed when the AGV moves in.
Observed behavior matches the training source description.
The timing is understood as intentional and tied to faster exchanges.
failure_conditions: Chute timing does not match the documented behavior in the source.
The observer cannot determine chute timing relative to AGV movement.
Additional control logic, thresholds, or corrective actions are inferred without source support.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if chute timing does not match the documented behavior in the source.
Escalate for SME review if the observed behavior cannot be reconciled with the training explanation.
Do not propose corrective actions or timing thresholds unless supported by another approved source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference frame describing AGV exchange flow and the statement that the chute opens when the AGV moves in, not when it drops a tote.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: We open the chute when the AGV moves in, not when it drops a tote.

We have very strict quick KPI's we have to meet. So it's, it's not, you know, if if you see the light go off and it hasn't dropped its tote, that's on purpose because we're trying to speed up the exchanges.

missing_details: The source does not define the referenced light or provide a formal status mapping.
The source does not provide corrective actions if observed timing differs.
The source does not provide timing thresholds, control parameters, or quantitative KPI limits.
The source does not specify supporting roles beyond the operator-level observation context.
